paragraph: The single-family market in Hasbrouck Heights is a real seller's market right now. Over the last seven months, 31 homes sold at a median of $725,000, with nearly 58 percent selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.01. Median days on market sits at 21, which means priced homes are moving without sitting around. You're seeing multiple offer situations on well-presented properties, and sellers aren't under pressure to discount. The range is wide, from $360,000 to $980,000, so there's genuine diversity in what's available, but the speed and the ratio tell you that decent homes in this town are drawing real competition. Multi-family properties are too sparse in this market to read any meaningful pattern, with only three sales over seven months, so if you're looking at a two-to-four unit building, treat each one as a unique negotiation rather than a market condition. If you're looking at Hasbrouck Heights as a place to live, you're entering a market where you'll need to move decisively on the right property and price competitively if you want to sell down the line.
